explanation: The input natural language states that in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, the minimum expiratory time (min_exp_time_psv) must be exactly half of the last inspiration time, and must also always fall within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds. There are three relevant atomic propositions: PSVMode for being in PSV mode, minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 for verifying the correct calculation, minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4, and minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2 for the interval bounds. The most suitable template is to say that, globally in PSV mode, whenever PSV mode is active, both the equality to half and the interval constraints must hold. This can be expressed by conjuncting the three required conditions under the 'when PSVMode is active, minExpiratoryTime must be half, and in range'. This is captured by the 'Globally (If condition then always property)' template, using a boolean expression with all three constraints and PSVMode as the condition.

The property requires that whenever the PSV mode is selected (PSVModeSelected), the minimum expiratory time in PSV (min_exp_time_psv) must be set to half of the last inspiration time (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2), and it must stay within the interval of [0.4, 2] seconds (i.e., minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 and minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2 must both hold). This is captured as a global invariant (G), where PSVModeSelected implies that all three of these constraints are satisfied. The property reflects the requirement that, in PSV mode, min_exp_time_psv is always half the last inspiration time, and is bounded between 0.4 and 2 seconds, at every relevant state. N_DURATION is set to 3 as a sensible default for duration-limited templates, but is not used in this template.

The natural language requirement specifies that in PSV mode, the minimum expiratory time (min_exp_time_psv) should be set to half of the last inspiration time, and must also be within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds. There are atomic propositions directly indicating when min_exp_time_psv is set correctly ('minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'), and atomic propositions for the boundary conditions ('minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2'). We capture the requirement that whenever PSV mode is selected, min_exp_time_psv must be correctly set and within the allowed range. The LTL formula asserts that globally in every state where PSV mode is selected, all three conditions must hold. Therefore, the formula is: G (PSVModeSelected -> (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 & minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2)).

The natural language requirement states that, when PSV mode is active, the minimum expiratory time (min_exp_time_psv) must always (i.e., globally) be equal to half of the last inspiration time and be within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds. The property is formalized to require that whenever PSVMode is active, then both: minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 (min_exp_time_psv is half last inspiration time) holds, minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 (min_exp_time_psv \u2265 0.4s) holds, and minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2 (min_exp_time_psv \u2264 2s) holds. Using boolean AND to require all three must be true together when in PSVMode. Chosen is 'G (bool_exp1 \u2192 bool_exp3)', where bool_exp1 is PSVMode and bool_exp3 expresses the three required atomic propositions joined by AND. This ensures that the requirement is enforced at every time step during PSVMode.

The property requires that whenever the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode, then min_exp_time_psv must satisfy (1) being equal to half of the last inspiration time, and (2) min_exp_time_psv must remain between 0.4 and 2 seconds (inclusive). This is specified by using the PSVMode atomic proposition (indicating PSV is active) as the guard, and the conjunction of all constraints (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2, minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4, minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2) as the required property. Template 30 is chosen: 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', with bool_exp1 being 'PSVMode', and bool_exp3 being the conjunction of all 3 constraints. This captures the requirement from the natural language that these constraints must always hold during PSV mode.

This LTL property globally enforces that whenever PSV mode is selected and active (PSVModeSelected & PSVMode), it must always be true that min_exp_time_psv is set to half the last inspiration time (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2), and that min_exp_time_psv remains within the interval [0.4,2] seconds (minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2). This formalizes the requirement that, in PSV mode, the minimum expiratory time parameter must always be half the last inspiration and constrained within clinical safety bounds.

To express the requirement: 'In PSV mode, min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time. min_exp_time_psv shall be in the interval [0.4 : 2] sec.' We break it into two safety properties that should always hold when in PSV mode: (1) min_exp_time_psv is equal to half of the last inspiration time, and (2) min_exp_time_psv is within [0.4, 2] seconds, i.e., greater than or equal to 0.4 and less than or equal to 2. We can encode that: Whenever PSVMode is true, (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2) AND (minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4) AND (minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2) are all true. This is a global implication template, stating that whenever PSVMode holds, all three subrequirements about min_exp_time_psv must hold. These are conjoined in bool_exp3. The template matches specification 17: G (bool_exp2 -> (G bool_exp3)), with bool_exp2 = PSVMode and bool_exp3 = (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 & minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2).

The property describes that whenever the PSV mode is active (using the 'PSVMode' atomic proposition), then it should always be the case that min_exp_time_psv is set to half of the last inspiration time (as indicated by 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'), and min_exp_time_psv also falls within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds (as indicated by 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2'). Since LTL templates only support boolean expressions over atomic propositions, this property encodes the requirements conjunctively whenever PSVMode is active. This accurately captures the natural language specification that in PSV mode, min_exp_time_psv must always be half of the last inspiration time and kept between 0.4 and 2 seconds.

This LTL property formalizes that whenever PSVMode (Pressure Support Ventilation mode) is active, it is always the case that the following three conditions hold: 1) min_exp_time_psv is set to exactly half of the last inspiration time (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2), 2) min_exp_time_psv is at least 0.4 seconds (minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4), and 3) min_exp_time_psv does not exceed 2 seconds (minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2). This captures the natural language requirement that, in PSV mode, the minimum expiratory time (min_exp_time_psv) must be half of the last inspiratory time and also constrained to the given interval. By using conjunction (AND) of the three relevant atomic propositions, the property ensures both the computation and the imposed limits are simultaneously enforced whenever PSVMode is active.

The natural language requirement states that, in PSV mode, 'min_exp_time_psv' must always equal half of the last inspiration time and must remain in the interval [0.4 : 2] seconds. The atomic propositions provide matching signals: 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2' (for the first part of the requirement), and 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' (for interval bounds). To capture this, we use the formula: PSVMode -> (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 & minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2), meaning: whenever PSV mode is active, min_exp_time_psv is set correctly and within range. Template 30 matches this implication structure.
